sections.paragraph:
f. & עֲשָׂרָה, עֲשֶׂרֶת m. ten [“Arabic عَشَرُ f. عَشَرَةُ m. Syr. ܥܣܰܪ f. ܥܰܣܪܳܐ m. Ethiop. ዕሥር፡ etc. Etymologists are mostly agreed that this word is formed from the idea of the conjunction of the ten fingers.” See Thes. p. 1078]; always with a pl. noun, Exo 27:12 Jos 22:14 2 Sa. 19:44 (in עֲשָׂרָה לֶחֶם 1Sa 17:17 there is an ellipsis of כִּכְּרֹות). Used for a round number, Gen 31:7 Job 19:3. Plur. עֲשָׂרֹות tens, decades, Exo 18:21 Deu 1:15.

Derived nouns are, עָשֹׂור, עֲשִׂירִי, עִשָּׂרֹון, מַעֲשֵׂר, denom. verb עָשַׂר. Other forms of the cardinal number itself are-
